rows.label: Fuel level
rows.description: Amount of fuel in the fuel tank

rows.label: Fuel low warning
rows.description: Sets the trigger point for the low fuel dash warning message.

rows.label: Roof ID light color
rows.description: Color of the ID light on the roof of the car.

rows.label: Rear wing angle
rows.description: Angle of attack of the rear wing. Increasing rear wing angle adds total downforce and shifts the aero balance rearward (more understeer) while increasing drag.

rows.label: Heave spring
rows.description: The Heave spring is a center element that provides linear stiffness in pitch motion but not in roll; stiffer springs control the chassis attitude better (less pitch change) which is good for aerodynamics and camber control, but mechanical grip is often better with softer springs

rows.label: Heave spring
rows.description: The Heave spring is a center element that provides linear stiffness in pitch motion but not in roll; stiffer springs control the chassis attitude better (less pitch change) which is good for aerodynamics and camber control, but mechanical grip is often better with softer springs

rows.label: Torsion bar O.D.
rows.description: How stiff the spring is; to get the same ride heights after a spring change the spring perch must be adjusted; stiffer springs control the chassis attitude better (less roll or pitch change) which is good for aerodynamics and camber control, but mechanical grip is often better with softer springs; front springs can only be changed in axle pairs

rows.label: Torsion bar O.D.
rows.description: How stiff the spring is; to get the same ride heights after a spring change the spring perch must be adjusted; stiffer springs control the chassis attitude better (less roll or pitch change) which is good for aerodynamics and camber control, but mechanical grip is often better with softer springs; front springs can only be changed in axle pairs

rows.label: Pushrod length delta
rows.description: Adjusts the lengths of both front pushrods; used to adjust ride height without changing the deflection of the heave spring

rows.label: Pushrod length delta
rows.description: Adjusts the lengths of both rear pushrods; used to adjust ride height without changing the deflection of the heave spring

rows.label: Heave perch offset
rows.description: Adjusts the static loading of the Heave spring; used to adjust ride height

rows.label: Heave perch offset
rows.description: Adjusts the static loading of the heave spring; used to adjust ride height

rows.label: Torsion bar turns
rows.description: Adjusts the preload of the torsion bar; used to adjust ride height; make preload changes in pairs: same change for both front wheels, both left wheels, or LR/RF, RR/LF diagonals

rows.label: Torsion bar turns
rows.description: Adjusts the preload of the torsion bar; used to adjust ride height; make preload changes in pairs: same change for both front wheels, both left wheels, or LR/RF, RR/LF diagonals

rows.label: Torsion bar turns
rows.description: Adjusts the preload of the torsion bar; used to adjust ride height; make preload changes in pairs: same change for both front wheels, both left wheels, or LR/RF, RR/LF diagonals

rows.label: Torsion bar turns
rows.description: Adjusts the preload of the torsion bar; used to adjust ride height; make preload changes in pairs: same change for both front wheels, both left wheels, or LR/RF, RR/LF diagonals

rows.label: ARB size
rows.description: Changing the anti-roll bar size equates to a change to the ARB diameter; more ARB rate = more understeer; A is softest, E is stiffest

rows.label: ARB size
rows.description: Changing the anti-roll bar size equates to a change to the ARB diameter; more ARB rate = more oversteer; A is softest, E is stiffest

rows.label: ARB blades
rows.description: Stiffer anti-roll bar blade position = stiffer effective anti-roll bar = more understeer; P1 = softest setting, P5 = stiffest setting

rows.label: ARB blades
rows.description: Stiffer anti-roll bar blade position = stiffer effective anti-roll bar = more understeer; P1 = softest setting, P5 = stiffest setting

rows.label: Toe-in
rows.description: Toe-in means the left and right wheels are steered toward each other, negative toe-in (toe-out) means the left and right wheels are steered away from each other; stick to toe-in at the rear; less rear toe-in = less temperature buildup; more rear toe-in = more straight line stability

rows.label: Toe-in
rows.description: Toe-in means the left and right wheels are steered toward each other, negative toe-in (toe-out) means the left and right wheels are steered away from each other; stick to toe-in at the rear; less rear toe-in = less temperature buildup; more rear toe-in = more straight line stability

rows.label: Toe-in
rows.description: Toe-in means the left and right wheels are steered toward each other, negative toe-in (toe-out) means the left and right wheels are steered away from each other; stick to toe-out at the front; less front toe-out = less temperature buildup, more front toe-out = better turn-in with reduced straight line stability

rows.label: Brake pressure bias
rows.description: % of braking force acting at the front wheels; more front bias = car more stable under braking, but longer braking distances needed; too little front bias = car wants to spin under braking

rows.label: Front master cyl.
rows.description: Brake master cylinders convert foot pedal force into hydraulic brake line pressure; larger front master cylinders reduce front brake line pressure and move the brake balance rearward; smaller front master cylinders increase brake line pressure and move the brake balance forward; Low downforce configurations may require larger master cylinders, whereas higher downforce configurations may require smaller ones.

rows.label: Rear master cyl.
rows.description: Brake master cylinders convert foot pedal force into hydraulic brake line pressure; larger rear master cylinders reduce rear brake line pressure and move the brake balance forward; smaller rear master cylinders increase brake line pressure and move the brake balance rearward; Low downforce configurations may require larger master cylinders, whereas higher downforce configurations may require smaller ones.

rows.label: Pad compound
rows.description: Depending on the amount of downforce the car is producing, based on downforce trim, the car's braking performance can be altered to suit; with more downforce high levels of braking force can be utilized to slow the car down more effectively

rows.label: Bias migration
rows.description: Brake bias migration as a function of pedal position. Each position corresponds to a migration map which applies a 1% offset to brake bias at 0% brake pedal input before ramping to the brake pressure bias value at 100% brake pedal input. 1 results in no migration. 2 through 6 migrate bias in various ways. P2 is linear between 0-70%, P3 is non-linear between 0-100%, P4 is linear between 10-40%, P5 is linear between 20-80% and P6 is linear between 0-100%.

rows.label: Bias migration gain
rows.description: Brake bias migration gain. Applies a multiplier to the base migration map in 0.25% increments. For example, a setting of -4 will shift the effective bias rearwards 4% at 0% brake pedal input and at 100% brake pedal input the bias will be equal to the brake pressure bias setting. Ramping shape between 0-100% will depend upon the selected Bias migration setting.

rows.label: Gear stack
rows.description: Two sets of gear ratios are available for this car: short and tall. Choose based primarily on the maximum end-of-straight speed at this track.

rows.label: Preload
rows.description: Preload sets an offset amount of static differential locking force that will always be present during both acceleration and deceleration; more preload = more locking, which adds understeer off-throttle under deceleration and adds oversteer under acceleration

rows.label: Preload
rows.description: Preload sets an offset amount of static differential locking force that will always be present during both acceleration and deceleration; more preload = more locking, which adds understeer off-throttle under deceleration and adds oversteer under acceleration

rows.label: Coast/drive ramp options
rows.description: Coast/drive ramp options affect how much force is produced to keep both side of the differential locked together during acceleration out of and deceleration into a corner; the more locking force, the more understeer

rows.label: Clutch friction plates
rows.description: Clutch plate plates multiply the amount of locking the differential can create; four plates have 2x the locking effort compared to two plates, six plates have 3x the locking effort compared to two plates and 1.5x as much as four plates, and so on

rows.label: Starting pressure
rows.description: Air pressure in tire as measured when the tire is heated with tire warmers (65C); lower pressure = more heat buildup, better grip at lower loads; higher pressure = less heat, better grip at higher loads

rows.label: Starting pressure
rows.description: Air pressure in tire as measured when the tire is heated with tire warmers (65C); lower pressure = more heat buildup, better grip at lower loads; higher pressure = less heat, better grip at higher loads

rows.label: Starting pressure
rows.description: Air pressure in tire as measured when the tire is heated with tire warmers (65C); lower pressure = more heat buildup, better grip at lower loads; higher pressure = less heat, better grip at higher loads

rows.label: Starting pressure
rows.description: Air pressure in tire as measured when the tire is heated with tire warmers (65C); lower pressure = more heat buildup, better grip at lower loads; higher pressure = less heat, better grip at higher loads

rows.label: LS comp damping
rows.description: Low speed compression damping primarily affects how quickly load is transferred onto this tire in transient handling when the shock is compressed. More low speed compression loads the tire more quickly. Min damping at 0 setting. Max damping at 40 setting.

rows.label: LS comp damping
rows.description: Low speed compression damping primarily affects how quickly load is transferred onto this tire in transient handling when the shock is compressed. More low speed compression loads the tire more quickly. Min damping at 0 setting. Max damping at 40 setting.

rows.label: LS comp damping
rows.description: Low speed compression damping primarily affects how quickly load is transferred onto this tire in transient handling when the shock is compressed. More low speed compression loads the tire more quickly. Min damping at 0 setting. Max damping at 40 setting.

rows.label: LS comp damping
rows.description: Low speed compression damping primarily affects how quickly load is transferred onto this tire in transient handling when the shock is compressed. More low speed compression loads the tire more quickly. Min damping at 0 setting. Max damping at 40 setting.

rows.label: HS comp damping
rows.description: High speed compression damping primarily affects how this corner of the car responds over bumps and curb strikes. Must be tuned to find optimum performance. Min damping at 0 setting. Max damping at 40 setting.

rows.label: HS comp damping
rows.description: High speed compression damping primarily affects how this corner of the car responds over bumps and curb strikes. Must be tuned to find optimum performance. Min damping at 0 setting. Max damping at 40 setting.

rows.label: HS comp damping
rows.description: High speed compression damping primarily affects how this corner of the car responds over bumps and curb strikes. Must be tuned to find optimum performance. Min damping at 0 setting. Max damping at 40 setting.

rows.label: HS comp damping
rows.description: High speed compression damping primarily affects how this corner of the car responds over bumps and curb strikes. Must be tuned to find optimum performance. Min damping at 0 setting. Max damping at 40 setting.

rows.label: HS comp damp slope
rows.description: The slope of the high speed compression damping curve is used in conjunction with the high speed damping level to adjust the damping curve shapes. Low slope combined with high level produces very digressive curves that control the aero platform and suit large curb strikes. High slope and low level produces linear curves that typically suit bumpier tracks. Min damping at 0 setting. Max damping at 11 setting.

rows.label: HS comp damp slope
rows.description: The slope of the high speed compression damping curve is used in conjunction with the high speed damping level to adjust the damping curve shapes. Low slope combined with high level produces very digressive curves that control the aero platform and suit large curb strikes. High slope and low level produces linear curves that typically suit bumpier tracks. Min damping at 0 setting. Max damping at 11 setting.

rows.label: HS comp damp slope
rows.description: The slope of the high speed compression damping curve is used in conjunction with the high speed damping level to adjust the damping curve shapes. Low slope combined with high level produces very digressive curves that control the aero platform and suit large curb strikes. High slope and low level produces linear curves that typically suit bumpier tracks. Min damping at 0 setting. Max damping at 11 setting.

rows.label: HS comp damp slope
rows.description: The slope of the high speed compression damping curve is used in conjunction with the high speed damping level to adjust the damping curve shapes. Low slope combined with high level produces very digressive curves that control the aero platform and suit large curb strikes. High slope and low level produces linear curves that typically suit bumpier tracks. Min damping at 0 setting. Max damping at 11 setting.

rows.label: LS rbd damping
rows.description: Low speed rebound damping primarily affects how quickly the shock extends in transient handling. More low speed rebound slows the rate at which the shock extends. Min damping at 0 setting. Max damping at 40 setting.

rows.label: LS rbd damping
rows.description: Low speed rebound damping primarily affects how quickly the shock extends in transient handling. More low speed rebound slows the rate at which the shock extends. Min damping at 0 setting. Max damping at 40 setting.

rows.label: LS rbd damping
rows.description: Low speed rebound damping primarily affects how quickly the shock extends in transient handling. More low speed rebound slows the rate at which the shock extends. Min damping at 0 setting. Max damping at 40 setting.

rows.label: LS rbd damping
rows.description: Low speed rebound damping primarily affects how quickly the shock extends in transient handling. More low speed rebound slows the rate at which the shock extends. Min damping at 0 setting. Max damping at 40 setting.

rows.label: HS rbd damping
rows.description: High speed rebound damping primarily affects how this corner of the car responds over bumps and curb strikes. Must be tuned to find optimum performance. Min damping at 0 setting. Max damping at 40 setting.

rows.label: HS rbd damping
rows.description: High speed rebound damping primarily affects how this corner of the car responds over bumps and curb strikes. Must be tuned to find optimum performance. Min damping at 0 setting. Max damping at 40 setting.

rows.label: HS rbd damping
rows.description: High speed rebound damping primarily affects how this corner of the car responds over bumps and curb strikes. Must be tuned to find optimum performance. Min damping at 0 setting. Max damping at 40 setting.

rows.label: HS rbd damping
rows.description: High speed rebound damping primarily affects how this corner of the car responds over bumps and curb strikes. Must be tuned to find optimum performance. Min damping at 0 setting. Max damping at 40 setting.

rows.label: Camber
rows.description: Inward (negative) or outward (positive) lean of the wheel; more lean = more cornering force in direction of lean, more wear and heat, less braking capability

rows.label: Camber
rows.description: Inward (negative) or outward (positive) lean of the wheel; more lean = more cornering force in direction of lean, more wear and heat, less braking capability

rows.label: Camber
rows.description: Inward (negative) or outward (positive) lean of the wheel; more lean = more cornering force in direction of lean, more wear and heat, less braking capability

rows.label: Camber
rows.description: Inward (negative) or outward (positive) lean of the wheel; more lean = more cornering force in direction of lean, more wear and heat, less braking capability

rows.label: Hybrid rear drive enabled
rows.description: Sets whether the rear drive cornering mode is enabled

rows.label: Hybrid rear drive corner pct
rows.description: Sets the amount of rear drive when the car is cornering and can deploy with the MGU-K

rows.label: Traction control slip
rows.description: Setting for how aggressively the TC tries to control slip. Higher = intervention at smaller differences in LR & RR wheel speeds (increased TC activation sensitivity).

rows.label: Traction control gain
rows.description: Gain on the magnitude of TC intervention. Higher = more aggressive torque cut (larger reduction in engine torque once TC slip threshold has been reached)

rows.label: Fuel target
rows.description: Fuel use target per lap

rows.label: Front RH at speed
rows.description: As the car travels on the race track its ride heights change; front ride height at speed represents a way to understand how on-track front ride height affect the car aerodynamically using the 'Aero Calculator'; make front ride height changes in conjunction with rear ride height changes; use telemetry to find out the ride heights on different parts of the race track

rows.label: Rear RH at speed
rows.description: As the car travels on the race track its ride heights change; rear ride height at speed represents a way to understand how on-track rear ride height affect the car aerodynamically using the 'Aero Calculator'; make rear ride height changes in conjunction with front ride height changes; use telemetry to find out the ride heights on different parts of the race track

rows.label: Tire type
rows.description: Dry tires are slick (untreaded), and are intended for use on dry track surfaces. Wet tires are treaded, and are intended for use on wet track surfaces. In changeable conditions, you'll have to apply judgment over the crossover point between dries and wets
